how-it-works-p-6: Discover the clinical decision logic and neural networks that differentiate a simple sprain from a bone fracture.
how-it-works-p-7: When you answer clinical questions about pain level, swelling, bruising, and mobility loss, our backend processes the inputs using a **Random Forest Classifier** machine learning model.
how-it-works-p-9: Imagine consulting 200 doctors. Each doctor makes a decision by asking a series of yes/no questions (a decision tree). The final diagnosis is decided by a majority vote.
how-it-works-p-11: The model has learned that some signs (like visible bone deformity or sharp pain on bone pressure) are extremely high indicators of a fracture, while others (like general mild swelling) point towards a sprain.
how-it-works-p-13: Pain intensity · Bruising · Obvious deformity · Loss of movement · Point tenderness · Weight-bearing capability · Mechanism of injury
how-it-works-p-14: When you upload an X-ray scan, our system uses a **Convolutional Neural Network (CNN)**. This is a deep learning model specifically designed to read and analyze images.
how-it-works-p-16: The CNN scans the image pixel-by-pixel, applying special mathematical filters to highlight borders, textures, and lines in the bone. It checks if there are dark breaks or fractures in the highlighted edges.
how-it-works-p-18: A typical AI might check if the prediction is over 50%. Our model is calibrated to classify a scan as a fracture if the sigmoid value drops below the strict threshold of **0.002**, capturing even faint hairline cracks.
how-it-works-p-20: Input size: 128x128 pixels · Layer types: Convolutional (feature extractor) & Pooling (reducer) · Output: Sigmoid probability (fracture vs. normal)
how-it-works-p-22: You fill out symptoms or drop an X-ray image into the triage box.
how-it-works-p-24: Images are resized to 128x128 and normalized so lighting doesn't confuse the AI.
how-it-works-p-26: The RandomForest evaluates symptom trees; the CNN reads pixel borders.
how-it-works-p-28: Results are translated to safety levels (e.g. Red for Fracture Emergency, Green for Sprain).
how-it-works-p-29: Our models are highly trained but provide **preliminary triage estimates** for guidance only. Hairline fractures can be extremely subtle, and swelling can disguise deeper tissue tears. **Never ignore clinical pain.** Always consult a certified radiologist or medical practitioner for an official diagnosis.

privacy-policy-p-13: FractureOrSprain? collects the following types of information:
privacy-policy-p-15: We implement industry-standard security measures to protect your health information:
privacy-policy-p-16: Our service uses artificial intelligence and machine learning models to analyze your symptoms. Your anonymized data may be used to train and improve these models, but no personally identifiable information is retained in our training datasets.
privacy-policy-p-17: We use the following third-party services:
privacy-policy-p-19: Session data is temporarily stored during your assessment and automatically cleared when you close your browser. We do not maintain long-term records of individual assessments unless you explicitly consent to save your results.
privacy-policy-p-20: Our service is not intended for children under 13 years of age. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children. If you believe we have collected information from a child, please contact us immediately.
privacy-policy-p-21: We may update this Privacy Policy periodically. Changes will be posted on this page with an updated "Last Updated" date. Continued use of our service after changes indicates acceptance of the updated policy.
privacy-policy-p-22: If you have questions about this Privacy Policy or how we handle your data, please contact us at:

privacy-policy-li-27: Usage Data: Information about how you interact with our service, including pages visited and features used

hipaa-compliance-p-21: Understanding Health Information Privacy and Security
hipaa-compliance-p-22: The Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA) is a federal law enacted in 1996 that establishes national standards to protect sensitive patient health information from being disclosed without the patient's consent or knowledge.
hipaa-compliance-p-23: HIPAA applies to covered entities (healthcare providers, health plans, and healthcare clearinghouses) and their business associates who handle protected health information (PHI).
hipaa-compliance-p-24: For comprehensive information about HIPAA regulations, visit the official U.S. Department of Health & Human Services website:
hipaa-compliance-p-25: Establishes national standards for protecting individuals' medical records and personal health information. It gives patients rights over their health information and sets rules and limits on who can access and receive your health data.
hipaa-compliance-p-26: Requires appropriate administrative, physical, and technical safeguards to ensure the confidentiality, integrity, and security of electronic protected health information (ePHI).
hipaa-compliance-p-27: Requires covered entities to notify affected individuals, HHS, and in some cases the media, of breaches of unsecured protected health information.
hipaa-compliance-p-28: FractureOrSprain? is designed with privacy and security in mind. However, as a preliminary triage and educational tool that does not establish a doctor-patient relationship or provide formal medical diagnoses, we may not be classified as a HIPAA covered entity under certain circumstances.
hipaa-compliance-p-29: Regardless of regulatory classification, we are committed to protecting your health information using industry-standard security practices and HIPAA-aligned principles.
hipaa-compliance-p-30: All data transmitted between your device and our servers is encrypted using industry-standard SSL/TLS protocols. Health information is encrypted both in transit and at rest.
hipaa-compliance-p-31: We implement strict access controls to ensure that only authorized personnel can access protected health information on a need-to-know basis.
hipaa-compliance-p-32: We only collect the minimum necessary information to provide our service. Session data is temporary and not permanently stored unless you explicitly consent.
hipaa-compliance-p-33: Regular security assessments and vulnerability testing ensure our systems maintain high security standards aligned with HIPAA requirements.
hipaa-compliance-p-34: Data used for AI model training is anonymized and de-identified to prevent individual identification, following HIPAA de-identification standards.
hipaa-compliance-p-35: Inspired by HIPAA principles, we respect your rights to:
hipaa-compliance-p-36: In the unlikely event of a data breach involving your health information, we will:
hipaa-compliance-p-37: Learn about your rights under HIPAA
hipaa-compliance-p-39: If you have questions about how we protect your health information or our privacy practices, please contact us:
hipaa-compliance-li-42: Access: Request access to the information we have about you
hipaa-compliance-li-44: Deletion: Request deletion of your personal health information
hipaa-compliance-li-45: Restriction: Request restrictions on how we use your data
hipaa-compliance-li-46: Confidential Communication: Request communications through specific methods
hipaa-compliance-li-47: Accounting: Request an accounting of disclosures of your information
hipaa-compliance-li-48: Notify affected individuals within 60 days of discovery
hipaa-compliance-li-49: Provide detailed information about the breach and affected data
hipaa-compliance-li-50: Outline steps we're taking to address the breach
hipaa-compliance-li-51: Offer guidance on protective measures you can take
hipaa-compliance-li-52: Report to appropriate authorities as required by law
